patch.value: Telemetry queue oversized-event pruning not applied during flush phase (regression)

Telemetry events written before the 512KB size cap (commit 4ee31a2) accumulated in the queue as 3MB entries. The flush phase sent oversized events in batches, causing endpoint 422 rejections that also blocked valid events. Root cause: pruneExpiredQueueEntries only pruned by age/attempts, not by payload size. 60MB queue with 64 events (23 oversized). Fixed by adding serialized-size check in pruneExpiredQueueEntries.

Added oversized-event pruning in pruneExpiredQueueEntries: entries with serialized size > TELEMETRY_MAX_EVENT_BYTES (512KB) are now dropped during flush-phase prune, preventing them from blocking valid event flushes. Queue drained from 60MB/64 to 4.5MB/41 after first flush post-fix.
Fixed: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts pruneExpiredQueueEntries now drops oversized events. Build passes, 1087 tests at 100% coverage.
